entry.title: Disposing of "dust attack" UTXOs
entry.summary:
A new organizational structure has been established on GitHub under the [bip451](https://github.com/bip451) organization, which hosts repositories like [ddust](https://github.com/bip451/ddust) and [dusts](https://github.com/bip451/dusts). This centralization is intended to streamline the handling of issues and pull requests for these projects. In an effort to facilitate broader adoption, the licensing for these repositories has been updated to offer a choice between MIT or Apache 2.0 licenses, providing flexibility particularly for those requiring an explicit patent grant.
The roadmap for advancing BIP-451 from its current draft status towards full deployment involves several strategic goals. Key initiatives include enhancing test coverage and addressing any existing bugs within the `ddust` reference client and specification. Additionally, there is a focus on collecting extensive on-chain data related to dust attacks to deepen understanding of their impact and prevalence. Collaboration with wallet developers and library projects is essential to integrate BIP-451 support, which will help in effectively managing dust UTXOs in the bitcoin ecosystem.
Moreover, efforts are underway to launch an educational website dedicated to informing bitcoin users about BIP-451 and strategies for disposing of dust UTXOs. Tracking and reporting on the progress of eliminating main chain dust are also part of the ongoing tasks. Wallet developers who are interested in contributing to this initiative by integrating BIP-451 into their projects are encouraged to participate actively in this development phase.
